channel.title: Curvature on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Curvature on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Differential Geometry (3): The Shape Operator — Curvature of Surfaces
item.description: The previous article gave us the intrinsic apparatus: the first fundamental form $\mathrm{I}$ , encoded as the symmetric matrix $\begin{pmatrix}E & F \\ F & G\end{pmatrix}$ . With it, an ant on the surface can measure lengths, angles, and areas without ever leaving. What an ant on a cylinder cannot do, equipped only with $\mathrm{I}$ , is detect that the cylinder is bent. The cylinder has the same first fundamental form as the plane, yet sits very differently in $\mathbb{R}^3$ .
